title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Chills
intro: Herbal teas for Chills are a type of herbal remedy that aims to alleviate the symptoms of chills, such as shivering, sweating, and fever, through the use of various herbs and plant extracts. These teas can be effective in treating chills due to the antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties of the herbs used, which help to reduce body temperature, soothe the body, and prevent the spread of infection. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat chills include Peppermint tea, which helps to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea; Ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce fever; Echinacea tea, which boosts the immune system and helps the body fight off infection; Yarrow tea, which has antiseptic properties that can help to prevent the spread of infection; and Lemon balm tea, which has antiviral properties that can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Elderflower, Chamomile, and Sage can also be used to treat chills due to their calming and soothing effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment, also known as malaria or ague, due to their anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with the chills. By stimulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea teas can help to alleviate the chills and related symptoms, such as headache and fatigue. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat the chills ailment include a reduction in fever, relief from headache and fatigue, and a boost to the immune system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea.
Add the Echinacea mixture to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the Echinacea solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day when you have chills, and as needed for relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea purpurea teas for chills may be better than a placebo in some cases, as suggested by the results of 16 trials involving 3396 participants.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to help alleviate symptoms of the chills, but it may also cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take it in moderation, monitor your blood sugar levels if you have diabetes, and be cautious when consuming it with other herbal preparations or medications, especially those that affect blood clotting.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea teas for chills
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers per cup of hot water
Steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse
Drink 2-3 cups of Echinacea tea per day to help alleviate chills
Continue usage for 5-7 days to see optimal results

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment due to their warming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to increase blood flow and reduce discomfort. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, have been found to have analgesic and antipyretic effects, making it an effective remedy for the chills. By consuming ginger tea, individuals can experience relief from the aching and fever associated with the chills, as well as a sense of warmth and comfort. Regular consumption of ginger tea has also been shown to have additional benefits, such as aiding digestion and reducing nausea, further supporting its use as a treatment for the chills.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root or 2-3 pieces of fresh ginger.
Wash the ginger root with clean water and chop it into small pieces if using fresh ginger.
Boil the cup of water in a pot and add the chopped ginger or dried root.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the ginger's flavor and nutrients.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey or sugar to taste. Drink warm to help relieve chills.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas for chills may be beneficial as the methanolic extract of Z. officinale enhanced the antimicrobial potency of berberine, potentially helping to alleviate chills caused by bacterial infections such as Salmonella typhimurium.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat the chills ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with small doses, gradually increase as needed, and avoid taking Zingiber officinale teas on an empty stomach or before engaging in physical activities.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger per cup of boiling water.
Adjust dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if chills persist.
Continue using Zingiber officinale tea for 2-3 days.
Monitor chills and adjust dosage or usage if necessary.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment, a condition characterized by fever, sweating, and shivering. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of Ginkgo biloba teas help to reduce the severity of the chills, while its ability to improve blood circulation and oxygenation of tissues promotes overall recovery. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, work synergistically to modulate the immune response and alleviate symptoms of the chills. By promoting relaxation and reducing fever, Ginkgo biloba teas provide relief from the discomfort and distress associated with this ailment, making it a popular herbal remedy for its treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils, then turn off the heat.
Add the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea hot or warm to help relieve chills, and repeat as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists", Ginkgo biloba teas for chills may be potentially safe based on the information provided, as Ginkgo biloba is listed among the potentially safe herbs in the study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat the chills ailment, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To safely use Ginkgo biloba teas, it's essential to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming them before surgery or when taking blood-thinning medications, as they may interact with these substances.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in 1 cup boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to reduce chills.
Start with a small dosage of 1 teaspoon and increase to 2 teaspoons if needed.
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable results.
Monitor your body's response and adjust the dosage or frequency as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve symptoms such as fever, chills, and body aches. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including eucalyptol, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and providing antioxidant protection. By consuming Eucalyptus globulus teas, individuals can experience relief from the chills ailment as the herbal preparation helps to break down mucus, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat the chills ailment include a rapid reduction in symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a natural approach to managing the condition without the need for pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Eucalyptus globulus leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea hot or warm to help relieve chills and congestion.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating the chills, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat the chills, as overconsumption may lead to an imbalance of essential nutrients and dehydration should be monitored.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day
Start with 250-500 mg of dried leaves per cup
Adjust dosage to 500-1000 mg per cup if needed
Drink tea for 3-5 days to help reduce chills
Consult doctor if chills persist or worsen

Strain the tea and serve hot to help with chills. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Metabolism open, Camellia sinensis teas for chills may be beneficial due to the potential antiviral actions of Camellia sinensis, specifically its citrus species, which could help inhibit SARS-CoV-2 replication and entry to host cells.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can help alleviate the symptoms of chills, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions, particularly in individuals with pre-existing conditions or sensitive stomachs. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to drink Camellia sinensis teas in moderation, avoid mixing them with other herbal preparations, and be cautious when consuming them on an empty stomach.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment due to their thermogenic properties, which help to increase the body's temperature and alleviate symptoms. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as cinnamaldehyde, cinnamic acid, and eugenol that help to stimulate blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and improve immune function. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat the chills ailment include relief from fever, headache, and body aches, as well as improved digestion and reduced risk of complications. By incorporating Cinnamomum verum teas into their treatment regimen, individuals can effectively manage their symptoms and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 Cinnamomum verum stick, about 2 inches long.
Crush the stick into small pieces using a spoon or a mortar.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on a stove.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of crushed Cinnamomum verum to the boiling water.
Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study on Sairei-To (also known as TJ-114), which includes Cinnamomi cortex (Cinnamomum verum) as one of its components, Sairei-To has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be used to treat chills, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat chills, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it if you have any underlying medical conditions, such as bleeding disorders or diabetes.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment due to their warming properties, which help to counteract the cold and discomfort associated with this condition. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng teas, including ginsenosides and other saponins, play a crucial role in treating the chills by stimulating the body's natural response to cold stress and promoting the circulation of blood. The ginsenosides in Panax ginseng teas help to increase the production of heat in the body, while also reducing inflammation and improving overall health. By incorporating Panax ginseng teas into their daily routine, individuals may experience relief from the chills and other associated symptoms, making it a popular herbal remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 5-10 grams of dried Panax ginseng root.
Add ginseng root to a tea infuser or directly to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate chills.
preparations.preparation_study: According to International archives of allergy and applied immunology, Panax ginseng teas for chills may help alleviate symptoms due to their ability to inhibit phytohaemagglutinin (PHA-P)-induced transformation of peripheral blood lymphocytes in a dose-related manner.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be effective in treating the chills ailment, but some possible side effects may include insomnia, restlessness, and rapid heartbeat in individuals who consume high doses or are sensitive to the herb. Precautions should be taken when using Panax ginseng teas, such as avoiding high doses, especially in children, pregnant women, and individuals with high blood pressure or anxiety disorders, to minimize potential side effects like increased heart rate, blood pressure, and gastrointestinal issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal, have been used for centuries to treat the chills ailment due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of goldenseal, including berberine and hydrastine, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause the chills, while also reducing fever and relieving body aches. The antiseptic properties of goldenseal help to promote the healing of the body and reduce the severity of the chills, making it a popular herbal remedy for this ailment. The benefits of using goldenseal teas to treat the chills include reduced recovery time, alleviation of symptoms, and improved immune function, making it a valuable natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Hydrastis canadensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed to relieve chills.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat the chills ailment, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to the high berberine content. Precautions include using this herbal preparation in moderation and not consuming it for extended periods of time to minimize the risk of these side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the chills ailment due to their antispasmodic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms. The bioactive constituents present in Salvia officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid and camphor, possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to their therapeutic effects in treating the chills. By consuming Salvia officinalis teas, individuals can experience relief from the chills as the herbal preparation helps to stimulate circulation, reduce fever, and alleviate muscle spasms. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat the chills include rapid relief from symptoms, improved immune function, and a reduced risk of complications associated with the chills ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 2 cups of boiling water, and a teapot.
Chop the leaves finely and place them in the teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature to help relieve chills.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for chills may be beneficial due to its ability to revert resistance to gentamicin in Staphylococcus aureus when used as an adjuvant for antibiotic treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat the chills ailment, however, it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps due to its active compounds like thujone and rosmarinic acid. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Salvia officinalis teas in moderation, start with small amounts, and avoid using them during pregnancy, breastfeeding, or in individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies.

preparation_study: According to Archives of internal medicine, Echinacea purpurea teas for chills should not be used beyond 8 weeks to avoid hepatotoxicity, and patients taking other known hepatoxic drugs should avoid using it altogether.
intro_study: According to "Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y)", teas for chills, such as Echinacea Plus, have been found to be effective in relieving symptoms of cold or flu, reducing the duration of illness, and resulting in no negative effects.
